title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's 10th-inning walk-off hit led the Braves to a 3-2 win over the Red Sox.
Boston's loss drops their record to 18-26 due to struggles in scoring despite solid pitching.
Starting pitcher Connelly Early gave up two solo homers over five innings, detailing another strong performance.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs expresses interest in rejoining the Patriots, but a reunion is unlikely due to a pending A.J. Brown trade.
The Patriots' potential trade for A.J. Brown hinders their ability to sign Stefon Diggs, who had 1,045 yards last season.
Albert Breer suggests Patriots won't allocate cap space for Diggs alongside Brown and Romeo Doubs, impacting a possible reunion.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday afternoon near 965 Blue Hill Ave in Dorchester.
Boston police describe LaGrant-Gomes as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, with brown eyes and black hair.
Public urged to contact Boston Police with tips; anonymous tips accepted via CrimeStoppers and other channels.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, allegedly shot his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
The woman, 42, was critically injured, while the man suffered non-life-threatening wounds, according to Boston police.
Broomes is charged with assault with intent to murder and awaits a dangerousness hearing, held without bail.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Five former Red Sox pitchers, including Chris Sale and Nathan Eovaldi, have combined for a 2.74 ERA in 2026.
The 2026 lineup includes Mookie Betts, Alex Bregman, and Kyle Schwarber, who leads MLB in home runs this season.
Xander Bogaerts and Betts, key members of the 2018 World Series team, continue to perform well with their current teams.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony is off the wrist brace following a right wrist sprain and played catch on Friday.
Boston Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy says Anthony could begin swinging a bat soon after wrist progress.
Anthony, who has missed nine games, hopes to return soon but remains without a specific timeline for full activities.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally accelerated into BrightPath daycare in Norwood on Friday morning; no injuries were reported.
The driver failed to put the Jeep in park, causing it to roll forward and hit the accelerator.
The daycare at 1210 Providence Highway remains closed pending structural evaluation; damage was deemed moderate.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will finish the 2026 season with a 10-7 record, down from 14-3 in 2025.
The Patriots are facing a more challenging 2026 schedule with opponents like Seattle, Denver, and Chicago, affecting their win predictions.
Most outlets, including The Athletic, ESPN, and FOX Sports, foresee the Patriots securing a second-place AFC East finish and a playoff spot.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Marty, Life is Short,' featuring Martin Short and Catherine O'Hara, is now streaming on Netflix.
'Mars Attacks!' is available for free on YouTube and Plex.
Season 2 of 'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' is now streaming on Netflix.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu proposed a $4.9 billion budget with minimal spending increases due to financial constraints.
Several Boston City Council members consider rejecting Wu's budget, seeking increased spending and fewer cuts.
The council must decide on Wu's budget by June 10; failure to act results in automatic approval.
